nodes.name: Foundations
nodes.description: Core technical building blocks of AI: architecture, training paradigms, and scaling.

nodes.name: Intelligence & Learning
nodes.description: Cognitive capabilities, reasoning mechanisms, and learning paradigms driving AI intelligence.

nodes.name: Agency & Systems
nodes.description: Autonomous agent architectures, knowledge infrastructure, and tools enabling AI to act.

nodes.name: Applied Domains
nodes.description: Domain-specific AI deployments across robotics, software, science, and creative media.

nodes.name: Reliability & Understanding
nodes.description: Safety, alignment, interpretability, and evaluation ensuring AI behaves as intended.

nodes.name: AI Ecosystem
nodes.description: Business models, market dynamics, competitive landscape, and investment context of AI.

nodes.name: Pretraining & Scaling
nodes.description: How foundation models are trained at scale: data curation, compute scaling laws, and pretraining paradigms.

nodes.name: Model Architecture
nodes.description: Design of neural architectures including attention alternatives, efficiency innovations, and representational structure.

nodes.name: Post-Training Methods
nodes.description: Techniques applied after pretraining: finetuning, distillation, synthetic data, and test-time adaptation.

nodes.name: Reasoning & Planning
nodes.description: Chain-of-thought, test-time compute scaling, search algorithms, and mathematical problem-solving.

nodes.name: Reinforcement Learning
nodes.description: RL methods for LLM capability development: RLVR for reasoning, reward modeling, policy optimization, and training dynamics.

nodes.name: Multimodal Models
nodes.description: Vision-language, unified multimodal, and audio-speech models enabling cross-modal understanding and generation.

nodes.name: Agent Systems
nodes.description: Autonomous agent architectures for software engineering, computer use, multi-agent coordination, and self-improvement.

nodes.name: Knowledge & Memory
nodes.description: Agent memory architectures, retrieval-augmented generation, and context engineering for knowledge-intensive tasks.

nodes.name: Generative Media
nodes.description: Image, video, and world model generation including interactive environments and creative content production.

nodes.name: Robotics & Embodied AI
nodes.description: Vision-language-action models, robot learning, and spatial intelligence for physical-world AI systems.

nodes.name: Code & Software AI
nodes.description: AI for code generation, software engineering automation, and AI-assisted development workflows.

nodes.name: Scientific & Medical AI
nodes.description: AI for scientific discovery, hypothesis generation, medical diagnosis, and biological modeling.

nodes.name: Alignment & Safety
nodes.description: Methods ensuring AI behaves safely: alignment training, hallucination mitigation, and AI governance.

nodes.name: Interpretability
nodes.description: Mechanistic analysis of model internals, behavioral characterization, and understanding of AI computation.

nodes.name: Evaluation & Benchmarks
nodes.description: Benchmark design, evaluation methodology, saturation detection, and agent capability assessment.

nodes.name: AI Market Dynamics
nodes.description: Competitive landscape, model commoditization, open-source dynamics, and hardware infrastructure economics.

nodes.name: AI Business & Economics
nodes.description: Vertical AI products, SaaS disruption, AI pricing models, and the economics of deploying AI at scale.

nodes.name: Startup & Investment
nodes.description: VC funding dynamics, startup formation strategies, competitive moats, and investment frameworks for AI.

nodes.name: Scaling Laws
nodes.description: Empirical and theoretical relationships between training compute, data, parameters, and capability including inference-time scaling axes.

nodes.name: Pretraining Data
nodes.description: Data curation pipelines, quality filtering, synthetic pretraining corpora, and data scaling strategies for foundation models.

nodes.name: Continual Learning
nodes.description: Lifelong learning methods, catastrophic forgetting prevention, and approaches for ongoing model adaptation without degradation.

nodes.name: Transformer Alternatives
nodes.description: SSMs, Mamba, hybrid architectures, and non-transformer sequence models offering alternatives to standard attention.

nodes.name: Long-Context & Attention
nodes.description: Attention mechanisms and memory-augmented architectures enabling efficient very long context processing.

nodes.name: Adaptive Computation
nodes.description: Mixture-of-experts, conditional compute, and architectures that allocate compute dynamically based on input difficulty.

nodes.name: Representation Learning
nodes.description: Embedding architectures, JEPA-style joint-embedding predictive models, and theories of universal representational convergence.

nodes.name: Finetuning & Distillation
nodes.description: Supervised finetuning, knowledge distillation, preference learning, and post-training pipelines for capability transfer.

nodes.name: Synthetic Data Generation
nodes.description: Automated generation of high-quality training data using models for both pretraining and post-training stages.

nodes.name: In-Context & Meta-Learning
nodes.description: Mechanics of in-context learning, meta-learning for rapid adaptation, and self-adaptive language model approaches.

nodes.name: Test-Time Learning
nodes.description: Inference-time adaptation including TTT, TTRL, and approaches that update model parameters or context on individual inputs.

nodes.name: Chain-of-Thought Mechanics
nodes.description: CoT reasoning mechanics, trace quality, reasoning length effects, and efficient reasoning process generation.

nodes.name: Test-Time Compute Scaling
nodes.description: Scaling inference compute through extended reasoning, repeated sampling, budget forcing, and compute-optimal search.

nodes.name: Latent & Continuous Reasoning
nodes.description: Reasoning in continuous latent space including Coconut, looped transformers, and recurrent depth approaches.

nodes.name: Search & Tree-Based Reasoning
nodes.description: MCTS, tree-of-thought, language agent tree search, and deliberate planning via structured search over reasoning paths.

nodes.name: Mathematical & Formal Reasoning
nodes.description: Theorem proving, olympiad-level mathematics, formal verification, and symbolic reasoning with language models.

nodes.name: RL for LLM Reasoning (RLVR)
nodes.description: RL with verifiable rewards eliciting reasoning including DeepSeek-R1, Kimi k1.5, self-play, and expert iteration.

nodes.name: Reward Modeling & Verification
nodes.description: Process and outcome reward models, generative reward models, critique models, and automated verification systems.

nodes.name: Policy Optimization Algorithms
nodes.description: PPO, GRPO, DPO, FlowRL, and algorithmic innovations for stable and efficient LLM policy optimization.

nodes.name: RL Theory & Training Dynamics
nodes.description: Theoretical analysis of RL for LLMs covering exploration, entropy dynamics, reward limits, and training stability.

nodes.name: Vision-Language Models
nodes.description: Perception-focused VLMs for visual understanding, document analysis, multimodal QA, and visual chain-of-thought reasoning.

nodes.name: Unified Multimodal Models
nodes.description: Single architectures integrating both multimodal understanding and generation across images, text, and other modalities.

nodes.name: Audio & Speech Models
nodes.description: Real-time voice dialogue, speech synthesis, audio-language models, and multimodal audio-visual understanding.

nodes.name: Software Engineering Agents
nodes.description: AI agents for code generation, debugging, repository-level tasks, and end-to-end software engineering automation.

nodes.name: Computer Use & GUI Agents
nodes.description: Agents operating web browsers, desktop GUIs, and arbitrary computer interfaces through visual grounding and action.

nodes.name: Multi-Agent Coordination
nodes.description: Architectures for coordinating multiple AI agents including hierarchical orchestration, peer-to-peer, and role specialization.

nodes.name: Tool Use & Agent Protocols
nodes.description: Function calling, tool-integrated reasoning, MCP, A2A protocol, and infrastructure for agent-world interaction.

nodes.name: Agent Self-Evolution
nodes.description: Self-improving agents, open-ended skill acquisition, and systems that autonomously expand their own capabilities.

nodes.name: Agent Memory Systems
nodes.description: Long-term and short-term memory architectures enabling AI agents to retain and retrieve accumulated experience.

nodes.name: Retrieval-Augmented Generation
nodes.description: RAG architectures, graph-based knowledge retrieval, and knowledge-augmented generation for grounded AI responses.

nodes.name: Context Engineering
nodes.description: Deliberate shaping of agent context windows, KV-cache optimization, and stateful context management for production agents.

nodes.name: Image Generation Models
nodes.description: Autoregressive and diffusion image generation, visual tokenization architectures, and high-resolution synthesis approaches.

nodes.name: Video & World Models
nodes.description: Video generation, interactive world simulators, physics-grounded world foundation models, and game-engine synthesis.

nodes.name: Creative Content Generation
nodes.description: AI for human animation, film production, music, and professional-quality creative media generation.

nodes.name: Vision-Language-Action Models
nodes.description: VLA models connecting visual perception and language understanding to robot action for generalist control.

nodes.name: Robot Learning
nodes.description: Imitation learning, sim-to-real transfer, dexterous manipulation, locomotion, and policy learning for physical robots.

nodes.name: Spatial & 3D Intelligence
nodes.description: Spatial reasoning, 3D scene understanding, world-grounded navigation, and geometric intelligence for embodied AI.

nodes.name: Code Generation
nodes.description: LLM-based code synthesis, program generation from natural language, and automated code completion systems.

nodes.name: AI Software Engineering
nodes.description: AI-driven full software development lifecycle including testing, refactoring, code review, and CI/CD automation.

nodes.name: AI for Scientific Discovery
nodes.description: Autonomous hypothesis generation, open mathematical problem solving, and AI-driven end-to-end research workflows.

nodes.name: Medical & Biology AI
nodes.description: AI for medical diagnosis, drug discovery, single-cell biology, protein modeling, and clinical decision support.

nodes.name: Alignment Methods
nodes.description: RLHF, constitutional AI, deliberative alignment, and techniques for producing reliably safe and beneficial AI behavior.

nodes.name: AI Governance & Policy
nodes.description: Regulatory frameworks, existential risk governance, power dynamics, and societal impact of advanced AI systems.

nodes.name: Hallucination & Reliability
nodes.description: Factual calibration, uncertainty quantification, hallucination detection, and reliability mitigation for deployed models.

nodes.name: Mechanistic Interpretability
nodes.description: Circuit-level analysis, feature attribution, thought tracing, and mechanistic understanding of AI model computation.

nodes.name: Model Behavior Analysis
nodes.description: Behavioral characterization, capability elicitation, jagged intelligence patterns, and persona analysis in deployed models.

nodes.name: Benchmark Design & Saturation
nodes.description: Creating robust evaluation benchmarks, addressing data contamination, saturation dynamics, and evaluation best practices.

nodes.name: Agent & Task Evaluation
nodes.description: Evaluation frameworks for agentic systems, tool use benchmarks, reasoning evals, and long-horizon task assessment.

nodes.name: Commoditization & Open Source
nodes.description: Open-source model dynamics, commoditization of frontier capabilities, and competitive implications of releases like DeepSeek.

nodes.name: Compute & Hardware
nodes.description: GPU market dynamics, semiconductor landscape, data center infrastructure investment, and compute economics.

nodes.name: Frontier Lab Competition
nodes.description: Competitive dynamics between leading AI labs, market share shifts, model release timing, and strategic positioning.

nodes.name: Vertical AI & SaaS Disruption
nodes.description: Domain-specific AI companies, disruption of incumbent SaaS vendors, and the AI-native product architecture shift.

nodes.name: AI Pricing & Business Models
nodes.description: Outcome-based pricing, token economics, unit economics of AI services, and business model innovation in the AI era.

nodes.name: VC & Startup Ecosystem
nodes.description: Venture capital investment patterns, startup formation dynamics, funding rounds, and investor frameworks for AI companies.

nodes.name: Startup Formation & Go-to-Market
nodes.description: Competitive moat building, go-to-market sequencing, market timing, and early-stage strategy for AI startups.
